mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as investors price in improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle or recession regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation periods consistently produce positive 20d forward returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 150,000 signals robust economic expansion and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show negative forward returns in > 50% of cases over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, leading to higher corporate profit expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Combined positive signals fail to predict positive returns with accuracy below 52% over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside stable or increasing federal funds rates indicates a restrictive monetary stance that dampens economic growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns but actual forward returns are positive in > 65% of cases during tightening regimes.
